summary: the 3 user message modes built from a REAL bridge turn via Source+IsDelta: raw tmux / non-streaming final / streaming (tools removed)
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.mode3_streaming:
Reading the build ID from the file.

agent_review.notes: THREE MESSAGE MODES built genuinely from a REAL bridge turn via Source+IsDelta (replaces the earlier draft that faked mode2==mode3 on a workbench stand-in). mode1 raw tmux = raw ANSI pane frames (Claude Code banner...) — the terminal view, available + separable. mode3 streaming = clean assistant text "Reading the build ID from the file.\nThe build ID is **BUILD_ID_f434e72abb3d**." — narration + answer, NO ANSI, NO tool activity (the bridge tool mcp__api-bridge__execute_shell_command was used but kept OUT of the message, since tool calls are a separate event type), coherent and human-readable. mode2 non-streaming = the final answer "The build ID is BUILD_ID_f434e72abb3d.", consistent with mode3 (streamed_matches_final=true). Correct narration->tool interleaving, real work done (build id read via the bridge). Clean.
